clinical.chief_complaints: Exertional chest discomfort and breathlessness on climbing stairs for the past 6 weeks, relieved by rest
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Mr. Ravichandran Pillai, a 58-year-old male, presents with retrosternal chest discomfort on exertion (climbing 1-2 flights of stairs) for the past 6 weeks, associated with mild breathlessness, relieved within 5-10 minutes of rest. No discomfort at rest. No radiation to arm or jaw at presentation. No associated diaphoresis or palpitations. Risk factors: hypertensive for 8 years on regular medication, non-diabetic. Referred by cardiologist after positive treadmill test (TMT) for coronary angiography, which confirmed significant single-vessel disease. Admitted for elective percutaneous coronary intervention.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Stable angina with significant single-vessel coronary artery disease (proximal LAD, 80% stenosis) confirmed on coronary angiography — ICD-10: I25.110
clinical.planned_procedure: Elective Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty (PTCA) with single Bare Metal Stent (BMS) to proximal LAD

examination_findings.general: Conscious, cooperative, well-built, no pallor, icterus, cyanosis, clubbing, or pedal oedema
examination_findings.cvs: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, no added sounds, regular rhythm
examination_findings.rs: Air entry bilateral, clear, no added sounds

investigations.result_summary: CBC: Hb 14.1 g/dL, WBC 7100/cumm, Platelets 2.5 lakh. RFT: Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, Urea 28 mg/dL. Lipid Profile: Total Cholesterol 218 mg/dL, LDL 142 mg/dL, HDL 38 mg/dL, Triglycerides 178 mg/dL. Blood Sugar Fasting: 102 mg/dL. Troponin I: Negative. PT/INR: 12.6 sec / 1.04.
Sinus rhythm, rate 82/min. No acute ST-T changes at rest. Old TMT (12/06/2026) showed ST depression at peak exercise, Bruce protocol Stage II, terminated due to chest discomfort.

Coronary Angiography (18/06/2026): Right dominant system. LAD — 80% stenosis in proximal segment, discrete lesion. LCX — mild luminal irregularities, no significant stenosis. RCA — normal. LVEF 58% on ventriculography. Single-vessel disease, proximal LAD, suitable for PTCA with BMS.
Chest X-Ray PA view: Normal cardiac size, no active cardiopulmonary disease.

clinical.past_medical_history: Hypertensive for 8 years, well controlled on Telmisartan 40mg OD. No diabetes. No prior cardiac events.
clinical.family_history: Father had myocardial infarction at age 62

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: Coronary angiography report confirming single-vessel LAD disease
Informed consent form signed by patient for PTCA

clinical.notes: Clean elective PTCA case — single-vessel proximal LAD disease confirmed on angiography, positive TMT, no contraindications. ASA Grade II (controlled hypertension). All pre-procedure investigations normal. Happy-path pre-auth expected to approve cleanly with no document gaps. Stage 3 discharge summary will be deliberately built incomplete (missing PMAM signature, final diagnosis at discharge, discharge advice, and doctor qualification) to test CLAIM_GAPS routing via Stage 3 Step 2 completeness check. All other Stage 3 elements (claim docs, LOS, deviations) should be clean.
